text: <sup>{The Book of}</sup> <sup>{Zephaniah}</sup> d <sup>{For the choir director: On my stringed instruments.}</sup> <sup>{The Imminent Destruction of Judah}</sup> d This message from the LORD came to Cushi's son Zephaniah, <sup>{The Heb. name Zephaniah means The Lord has treasured}</sup> the grandson of Gedaliah and great-grandson of Hezekiah's son Amariah, during the reign <sup>{Lit. in the days of}</sup> of Amon's son Josiah, king of Judah:
"I'll utterly sweep away everything from <sup>{Lit. from the face of}</sup> the land,"declares the Lord .

"I'll consume both human beings and animals I'll consume the birds of the sky, the fish in the sea, and the wicked along with their sin, <sup>{Lit. with the heap of rubble}</sup> when I eliminate human beings from the land,"declares the Lord .

"I will also stretch out my hand against Judah, and upon all inhabitants of Jerusalem. I'll wipe out every trace of Baal from this place, and the name <sup>{Or authority}</sup> of the pagan priests <sup>{Lit. the Chemarim ; i.e. idol worshiping priests}</sup> , along with my own <sup>{The Heb. lacks my own}</sup> priests.

I'll wipe out <sup>{The Heb. lacks I'll wipe out}</sup> those who worship the stars that they view <sup>{The Heb. lacks that they view}</sup> from their housetops, those who bow down and swear to the Lord and who also swear by Milcom, <sup>{I.e. the national idol of the Ammonites}</sup>
those who turn away from the Lord, don't seek the Lord , and never ask for his help. <sup>{Lit. and don't inquire of him}</sup> "

<sup>{The Approaching Day of the Lord}</sup> d Remain silent in the presence of the Lord G od, because the Day of the Lord approaches, and because the Lord has prepared a sacrifice for those whom he has invited to be consecrated.

"It will come about during <sup>{Lit. in the day of}</sup> the Lord's sacrifice that I'll punish the officials, <sup>{Lit. princes}</sup> the royal descendants, and all who wear foreign clothing. <sup>{Or wear cultic vestments}</sup>

At the same time, I'll punish every idol worshipper, <sup>{Lit. everyone who leaps over the threshold ; cf. 1Sam 5:5}</sup> especially those who are filling their master's temple with violence and deceit.

When all of this happens," <sup>{Lit. It will come about in that day}</sup> declares the Lord , "a loud shriek will come <sup>{The Heb. lacks will come}</sup> from the Fish Gate, and howling from the Mishneh <sup>{Or from The Second ; i.e. a section of Jerusalem}</sup> Quarter, along with great destruction from the hills."

<sup>{Divine Judgment on the Business Community}</sup> d "Wail, you who live in the market district, because all of the merchants will be crushed and all of their customers <sup>{Lit. all who carry silver}</sup> will be eliminated. <sup>{Lit. be cut off}</sup>

And it will come about that I will search Jerusalem with candles, <sup>{Or with oil lamps}</sup> punishing the self-satisfied and complacent, who say to themselves, «The Lord will do neither good nor evil.'
Therefore their possessions will be seized as plunder and their homes left in ruins. They may build houses, but they won't live in them. They may plant vineyards, but they won't drink their wine."

<sup>{Zephaniah's Description of the Day of the Lord}</sup> d "The great Day of the Lord approaches How it comes, hurrying faster and faster! The sound of the Day of the Lord there includes the bitter cry of the mighty soldier.
That day will be filled with wrath, a day of trouble and tribulation, a day of desolation and devastation, a day of doom and gloom, a day of clouds and shadows, <sup>{Cf. Joel 2:2a}</sup>
a day of trumpet and battle cry against fortified cites and watch <sup>{Lit. corner}</sup> towers.

"And I'll bring so much distress to people that they will walk around like the blind. Because they have sinned against the Lord , their blood will be poured out like dust and their intestines will spill out <sup>{The Heb. lacks will spill out}</sup> like manure.

Neither their silver nor their gold will deliver them in the Day of the Lord's wrath; but the entire land will be consumed by the fire of his jealousy, for he will bring the inhabitants of the land to a sudden end."
<sup>{A Plea for Repentance to the People}</sup> d "Gather together! Yes, indeed, gather together, you shameless nation!

Before the decree is carried out, before the day flies away like chaff, before the fierce anger of the Lord visits you, before the Day of the Lord's wrath surprises <sup>{Lit. wrath comes upon}</sup> you,
seek the Lord , all you humble people of the land, who do what he commands. Seek righteousness! Seek humility! Maybe you will be protected in the Day of the Lord's anger."

<sup>{The Coming Destruction of Philistine Cities}</sup> d "For Gaza will be forsaken, <sup>{MT word forsaken sounds like The Heb. name Gaza.}</sup> and Ashkelon deserted Ashdod will be emptied at high noon; even Ekron will be uprooted. <sup>{MT word uprooted sounds like The Heb. name Ekron .}</sup>

Woe to those who live along the coast, the people of Philistia! <sup>{Lit. of the Cherethites}</sup> This message from the Lord is being spoken against you, Canaan, land of the Philistines: «I'll destroy you until no one lives there!'
The Philistine <sup>{Lit. Cherethite}</sup> coast will become meadows for shepherds and sheep pens.

The survivors of Judah will find pasture on it; at twilight they will lie down in the houses of Ashkelon, for the Lord their God will visit them, restoring their prosperity." <sup>{Or them, turning away their captivity}</sup>

<sup>{The Lord's Rebuke to Moab and Ammon}</sup> d "I've heard Moab's insults and the curses from those Ammonites <sup>{Lit. the descendants of Ammon ; so also in v. 9.}</sup> by which they defame my people and boast about their territory.

Therefore as I'm alive and living," declares the Lord of the Heavenly Armies, the God of Israel, "Moab will surely become like Sodom, and the Ammonites <sup>{Lit. the descendants of Ammon ; so also in v. 9.}</sup> like Gomorrah, a place overrun by weeds and salty marshes, unpopulated forever. The survivors of my people will confiscate their property, and those who remain of my nation will inherit what was theirs. <sup>{Cf. Obad 19-21}</sup>
This they <sup>{I.e. the people of Edom and Moab}</sup> will have in lieu of their pride, because they have insulted and mocked the people of the Lord of the Heavenly Armies.
The Lord will incite them to terror, because he will cause all the gods of the earth to waste away. They will worship him, every person in his own home, including even the coastlands of the nations."
<sup>{The Lord's Rebuke to Cush and Nineveh}</sup> d "Now as for you, Cush, <sup>{I.e. Sudan, Ethiopia, or southern Iraq}</sup> you <sup>{Lit. they}</sup> will surely be slain by my sword!

"And the Lord <sup>{Lit. And he}</sup> will attack <sup>{Lit. stretch out his hand toward}</sup> the north, destroying Assyria. He will turn Nineveh into a desolate ruin, as dry as a desert wilderness.

Flocks will lie down in her midst, along with animals of every kind. Desert owls and screeching owls will nest at the top of the pillars, hooting through the vacant <sup>{The Heb. lacks vacant}</sup> windows, «Ruin sits at these doorsills,'for he will expose even the cedar framework.

This is that carefree city that lived irresponsibly, that told herself, «Me first!'<sup>{Lit. said in her heart, «I'}</sup> and, «There will be no one else!'How ruined she has become a habitat for wild animals! Everyone who passes by her will sneer at her and make obscene gestures!" <sup>{Lit. and wag his hand}</sup>
<sup>{The Lord's Rebuke to Jerusalem}</sup> d Woe to this filthy, polluted, and oppressive city!
It won't obey anyone. <sup>{Lit. obey the voice}</sup> It won't accept discipline. It does not trust in the Lord . It does not approach God.
Its national officials <sup>{Lit. Her princes}</sup> are roaring lions; its judges are like wolves of the night that don't leave the bones for the morning.
Its prophets are arrogant and treacherous. Its priests have contaminated the sanctuary. They give perverse interpretations of the Law. <sup>{Lit. They do violence to the Law.}</sup>
The righteous Lord who lives <sup>{The Heb. lacks within her}</sup> within her will do no wrong; he will bring justice to light morning by morning. He never fails, but the unjust are shameless.

<sup>{The Integrity of God's Justice}</sup> d "I have destroyed <sup>{Lit. have cut off}</sup> nations their fortifications are deserted. I have turned their main thoroughfares into wastelands where no one will travel. Their cities are desolate; as a result, not one man remains no, not even a single resident.

I have said, «If only you would fear me, if only you would take my instructions to heart.'Then their houses would not have been torn down. I have chastened them, but they were eager to corrupt everything they were doing."

<sup>{The Future Deliverance of Israel}</sup> d "Just you wait!" declares the Lord. "The day will come when I stand up as a prosecutor, <sup>{Or witness}</sup> for I am determined to assemble the nations and to gather the kingdoms, in order to pour out my indignation upon them all of my fierce anger. All the earth will be consumed by the fire of my jealousy.
Indeed, then I will return my people to a pure language <sup>{Or to pure lips}</sup> so that they all may call upon the name of the Lord, serving him with a united will. <sup>{Lit. shoulder}</sup>
"From beyond the rivers of Cush <sup>{I.e. Sudan, Ethiopia, or southern Iraq}</sup> my worshipers <sup>{Lit. incense burners}</sup> including my dispersed people will present offerings to me.

When this happens, <sup>{Lit. On that day}</sup> you will not be ashamed of your actions by which you sinned against me, because I will remove from among you those who revel in pride. Arrogance will have no place in my holy mountain.
I will keep a humble and gentle people among you, and they will trust in the name of the Lord .
The survivors of Israel will not practice lawlessness nor tell lies, nor will a deceitful message be found in their mouths, because they will eat and rest, with no one to cause fear."
<sup>{Israel's Future Joys}</sup> d "Sing aloud, daughter of Zion! Shout out, Israel! Rejoice with all of your heart, daughter of Jerusalem!
The Lord has acquitted you; <sup>{Lit. has withdrawn your judgments}</sup> turning back your adversaries. Israel's king, the Lord , is among you; you will not fear disaster anymore.
"When all of this happens, <sup>{Lit. In that day}</sup> it will be told Jerusalem, «Don't be afraid!" and to Zion, «Don't lose courage!" <sup>{Lit. Don't let your hands drop}</sup>

The Lord your God among you is powerful he will save and he will take joyful delight in you. In his love he will renew you <sup>{The Heb. lacks you}</sup> with his love; he will celebrate with singing because of you.
I will gather the afflicted from the solemn assembly; those who were with you, who were bearing a burden of disgrace.

"Watch how I deal with everyone who oppresses you! At that time I will rescue the one who is lame, and I will draw to me <sup>{The Heb. lacks to me}</sup> the one who has been driven away. I will honor <sup>{Lit. give}</sup> them with praise and with a good reputation in every land where they have been put to shame.

At that time I will gather you; at that time I will bring you home. <sup>{The Heb. lacks home}</sup> Indeed, I will give you a good reputation, making you praiseworthy among all of the people of the world, when I restore your prosperity before your eyes," says the Lord .
